```tactics
import Mathlib
import SpectralThm.RieszMarkovKakutani.Complex
import SpectralThm.Resolutions
-- import SpectralThm.WStarAlgebra.BorelFunctionalCalculus

/-!
# The Spectral Theorem

-/

@[expose] public section

open InnerProductSpace ContinuousLinearMap ZeroAtInfty ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap MeasureTheory

noncomputable section

variable {H : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup H] [InnerProductSpace ℂ H] [CompleteSpace H]
  {a : H →L[ℂ] H} (ha : IsSelfAdjoint a)

variable (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ))

/-- For `x y : H`, consider `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` as a bounded linear functional in `b`. -/
def toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : (H →L[ℂ] H) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL (H →L[ℂ] H) _ _ _ _ (innerSL ℂ x) (apply' H _ y)

@[simp]
lemma toLinearFunctional_apply (x y : H) {b : H →L[ℂ] H}:
    toLinearFunctional x y b = ⟪x, (b y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- The linear functional `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`. -/
lemma toLinearFunctional_bounded (x y : H) : ‖toLinearFunctional x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  grw [ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound]
  . positivity
  . intro a
    simp only [toLinearFunctional_apply]
    grw [norm_inner_le_norm, ContinuousLinearMap.le_opNorm]
    apply le_of_eq
    ring

/-- Let `a` a normal bounded operator. By the continuous functional calculus, there is an injective
homomorphism from `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)` to  `H →L[ℂ] H`, informally denoted by `f => f(a)`. This
definition just makes it a continuous linear map.
cf. Rudin "Functional Analysis" Theorem 11.18, the proof of Theorem 12.23, first paragraph.
-/
def cfcCLM : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ((cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toLinearMap).mkContinuous 1
        ( by simp only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul];
              exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

/-- The same map `f => f(a)` as a continuous algebra homomorphism.
-/
def cfcCAH : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →A[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ContinuousAlgHom.mk (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toAlgHom
        (by apply @continuous_of_linear_of_bound _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toAlgHom.map_add'
              (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toLinearMap.map_smul' 1
            simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe,
              AlgHom.toRingHom_toMonoidHom, OneHom.toFun_eq_coe, MonoidHom.toOneHom_coe,
              MonoidHom.coe_coe, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul]
            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

@[simp]
lemma cfcCLM_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) : cfcCLM ha f = cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f := rfl

/-- The composition of the linear functional and the continuous functional,
`f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` as a continuous linear functional. -/
def cfc_toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

@[simp]
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) (x y : H) :
    cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, the linear map `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ`has norm `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
This follows from the bound `toLinearFunctional_bounded` and the fact that
`f => f(a)` is an isometry, `norm_cfcHom`. -/
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound (x y : H) : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound
  · positivity
  · intro f
    simp only [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    calc
      ‖⟪x, ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y⟫_ℂ‖
        ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y‖ := norm_inner_le_norm x
          (((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ * ‖y‖) :=
        mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (le_opNorm ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
          (norm_nonneg x)
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ := by ring
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rw [norm_cfcHom a f]

functional `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` by the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem.
```

```tactics
    cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, the linear map `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ`has norm `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
This follows from the bound `toLinearFunctional_bounded` and the fact that
`f => f(a)` is an isometry, `norm_cfcHom`. -/
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound (x y : H) : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound
  · positivity
  · intro f
    simp only [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    calc
      ‖⟪x, ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y⟫_ℂ‖
        ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y‖ := norm_inner_le_norm x
          (((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ * ‖y‖) :=
        mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (le_opNorm ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
          (norm_nonneg x)
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ := by ring
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rw [norm_cfcHom a f]

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, one obtains a complex measure `E_{a, x ,y}` from the continuous linear
functional `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` by the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem.
cf. Rudin "Real and complex analysis" Theorem 6.19 (2), combined with the bound by
`cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound`.-/
def cfc_toComplexMeasure (x y : H) := ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)
  C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)

/-- The integral of `f` against `E_{a, x,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖`. -/
lemma cfc_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
  sorry

/-- The complex measure `E_{a, x,y}` has the total variation bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
Rudin "Real and complex analysis" Theorem 6.19 (2), combined with the bound by
`cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound`.-/
lemma cfc_variation_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) :
    (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := by
  sorry

/-- The integral of a Borel function `f` against `E_{a, x ,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
We should have an analogue of `MeasureTheory.lintegral_le_const` for `ComplexMeasure.integral`.
-/
lemma bfc_bounded (x y : H) {C : ℝ} {f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ}  (hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C)
    (hX : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
  sorry

/-- The linear functional from bounded Borel functions `f` to `∫ f z ∂E_{a, x, y} z` as a bounded
linear functional (bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`). All the remaining properties should follow from
the definition and `bfc_bounded`. -/
def toBoundedBilin {C : ℝ} (f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ)
  (hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C) (hf_measurable : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f)
  : H →L⋆[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] ℂ where
  toFun x := {  toFun y := (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f
                map_add' := sorry
                map_smul' := sorry
```

Now we need to define `toBoundedBilin` and prove its properties. Let's define it as a map from `H` to `H*` (adjointable operators from `H` to `ℂ`):

```tactics
import Mathlib
import SpectralThm.RieszMarkovKakutani.Complex
import SpectralThm.Resolutions
-- import SpectralThm.WStarAlgebra.BorelFunctionalCalculus

/-!
# The Spectral Theorem

-/

@[expose] public section

open InnerProductSpace ContinuousLinearMap ZeroAtInfty ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap MeasureTheory

noncomputable section

variable {H : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup H] [InnerProductSpace ℂ H] [CompleteSpace H]
  {a : H →L[ℂ] H} (ha : IsSelfAdjoint a)

variable (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ))

/-- For `x y : H`, consider `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` as a bounded linear functional in `b`. -/
def toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : (H →L[ℂ] H) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL (H →L[ℂ] H) _ _ _ _ (innerSL ℂ x) (apply' H _ y)

@[simp]
lemma toLinearFunctional_apply (x y : H) {b : H →L[ℂ] H}:
    toLinearFunctional x y b = ⟪x, (b y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- The linear functional `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`. -/
lemma toLinearFunctional_bounded (x y : H) : ‖toLinearFunctional x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  grw [ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound]
  . positivity
  . intro a
    simp only [toLinearFunctional_apply]
    grw [norm_inner_le_norm, ContinuousLinearMap.le_opNorm]
    apply le_of_eq
    ring

/-- Let `a` a normal bounded operator. By the continuous functional calculus, there is an injective
homomorphism from `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)` to  `H →L[ℂ] H`, informally denoted by `f => f(a)`. This
definition just makes it a continuous linear map.
cf. Rudin "Functional Analysis" Theorem 11.18, the proof of Theorem 12.23, first paragraph.
-/
def cfcCLM : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ((cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toLinearMap).mkContinuous 1
        ( by simp only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul];
              exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

/-- The same map `f => f(a)` as a continuous algebra homomorphism.
-/
def cfcCAH : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →A[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ContinuousAlgHom.mk (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toAlgHom
        (by apply @continuous_of_linear_of_bound _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toAlgHom.map_add'
              (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toLinearMap.map_smul' 1
            simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe,
              AlgHom.toRingHom_toMonoidHom, OneHom.toFun_eq_coe, MonoidHom.toOneHom_coe,
              MonoidHom.coe_coe, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul]
            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

@[simp]
lemma cfcCLM_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) : cfcCLM ha f = cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f := rfl

/-- The composition of the linear functional and the continuous functional,
`f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` as a continuous linear functional. -/
def cfc_toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

@[simp]
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) (x y : H) :
    cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, the linear map `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ`has norm `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
This follows from the bound `toLinearFunctional_bounded` and the fact that
`f => f(a)` is an isometry, `norm_cfcHom`. -/
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound (x y : H) : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound
  · positivity
  · intro f
    simp only [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    calc
      ‖⟪x, ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y⟫_ℂ‖
        ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y‖ := norm_inner_le_norm x
          (((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ * ‖y‖) :=
        mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (le_opNorm ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
          (norm_nonneg x)
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ := by ring
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rw [norm_cfcHom a f]

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, one obtains a complex measure `E_{a, x ,y}` from the continuous linear
functional `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` by the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem.
cf. Rudin "Real and complex analysis" Theorem 6.19 (2), combined with the bound by
`cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound`.-/
def cfc_toComplexMeasure (x y : H) := ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)
  C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)

/-- The integral of `f` against `E_{a, x,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖`. -/
lemma cfc_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
  sorry

/-- The complex measure `E_{a, x,y}` has the total variation bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
Rudin "Real and complex analysis" Theorem 6.19 (2), combined with the bound by
`cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound`.-/
lemma cfc_variation_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) :
    (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := by
  sorry

/-- The integral of a Borel function `f` against `E_{a, x ,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
We should have an analogue of `MeasureTheory.lintegral_le_const` for `ComplexMeasure.integral`.
-/
lemma bfc_bounded (x y : H) {C : ℝ} {f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ}  (hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C)
    (hX : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
  sorry

/-- The linear functional from bounded Borel functions `f` to `∫ f z ∂E_{a, x, y} z` as a bounded
linear functional (bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`). All the remaining properties should follow from
the definition and `bfc_bounded`. -/
def toBoundedBilin {C : ℝ} (f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ)
  (hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C) (hf_measurable : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f)
  : H →L⋆[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] ℂ where
  toFun x := {  toFun y := (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f
                map_add' := sorry
                map_smul' := sorry
```

The main goal is to prove that for fixed `x` and `x_1`, the map `m ↦ ∫ (m • x_1) dE_{a,x}` is linear. This means showing `∀ m, ∫ (m • x_1) dE_{a,x} = m • ∫ x_1 dE_{a,x}`.

Looking at the code, the main goal is:
`∀ (m : ℂ) (x_1 : H), ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H inst✝² inst✝¹ inst✝ a ha x (m • x_1)) f = (RingHom.id ℂ) m • ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H inst✝² inst✝¹ inst✝ a ha x x_1) f`

Let's think about how to prove this. The key insight is that `cfc_toComplexMeasure` is defined as `ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure` applied to a linear functional, and the integral should then behave linearly.

```tactics
import Mathlib
import SpectralThm.RieszMarkovKakutani.Complex
import SpectralThm.Resolutions
-- import SpectralThm.WStarAlgebra.BorelFunctionalCalculus

/-!
# The Spectral Theorem

-/

@[expose] public section

open InnerProductSpace ContinuousLinearMap ZeroAtInfty ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap MeasureTheory

noncomputable section

variable {H : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup H] [InnerProductSpace ℂ H] [CompleteSpace H]
  {a : H →L[ℂ] H} (ha : IsSelfAdjoint a)

variable (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ))

/-- For `x y : H`, consider `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` as a bounded linear functional in `b`. -/
def toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : (H →L[ℂ] H) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL (H →L[ℂ] H) _ _ _ _ (innerSL ℂ x) (apply' H _ y)

@[simp]
lemma toLinearFunctional_apply (x y : H) {b : H →L[ℂ] H}:
    toLinearFunctional x y b = ⟪x, (b y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- The linear functional `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`. -/
lemma toLinearFunctional_bounded (x y : H) : ‖toLinearFunctional x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  grw [ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound]
  . positivity
  . intro a
    simp only [toLinearFunctional_apply]
    grw [norm_inner_le_norm, ContinuousLinearMap.le_opNorm]
    apply le_of_eq
    ring

/-- Let `a` a normal bounded operator. By the continuous functional calculus, there is an injective
homomorphism from `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)` to  `H →L[ℂ] H`, informally denoted by `f => f(a)`. This
definition just makes it a continuous linear map.
cf. Rudin "Functional Analysis" Theorem 11.18, the proof of Theorem 12.23, first paragraph.
-/
def cfcCLM : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ((cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toLinearMap).mkContinuous 1
        ( by simp only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul];
              exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

/-- The same map `f => f(a)` as a continuous algebra homomorphism.
-/
def cfcCAH : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →A[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ContinuousAlgHom.mk (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toAlgHom
        (by apply @continuous_of_linear_of_bound _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toAlgHom.map_add'
              (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toLinearMap.map_smul' 1
            simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe,
              AlgHom.toRingHom_toMonoidHom, OneHom.toFun_eq_coe, MonoidHom.toOneHom_coe,
              MonoidHom.coe_coe, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul]
            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

@[simp]
lemma cfcCLM_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) : cfcCLM ha f = cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f := rfl

/-- The composition of the linear functional and the continuous functional,
`f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` as a continuous linear functional. -/
def cfc_toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

@[simp]
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) (x y : H) :
    cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, the linear map `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ`has norm `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
This follows from the bound `toLinearFunctional_bounded` and the fact that
`f => f(a)` is an isometry, `norm_cfcHom`. -/
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound (x y : H) : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound
  · positivity
  · intro f
    simp only [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    calc
      ‖⟪x, ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y⟫_ℂ‖
        ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y‖ := norm_inner_le_norm x
          (((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ * ‖y‖) :=
        mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (le_opNorm ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
          (norm_nonneg x)
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ := by ring
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rw [norm_cfcHom a f]

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, one obtains a complex measure `E_{a, x ,y}` from the continuous linear
functional `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` by the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem.
cf. Rudin "Real and complex analysis" Theorem 6.19 (2), combined with the bound by
`cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound`.-/
def cfc_toComplexMeasure (x y : H) := ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)
  C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y)
  (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE
  (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)

/-- The integral of a Borel function `f` against `E_{a, x,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
We should have an analogue of `MeasureTheory.lintegral_le_const` for `ComplexMeasure.integral`.
-/
lemma bfc_bounded (x y : H) {C : ℝ} {f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ}  (hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C)
    (hX : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
  sorry

/-- The linear functional from bounded Borel functions `f` to `∫ f z ∂E_{a, x,y} z` as a bounded
linear functional (bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`). All the remaining properties should follow from
the definition and `bfc_bounded`. -/
def toBoundedBilin {C : ℝ} (f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ)
  --(hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C) (hf_measurable : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f)
  : H →L⋆[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] ℂ where
  toFun x := {  toFun y := (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f
                map_add' := sorry
                map_smul' := sorry
```

Looking at the code, the main goal is:
`∀ (m : ℂ) (x_1 : H), ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H inst✝² inst✝¹ inst✝ a ha x (m • x_1)) f = (RingHom.id ℂ) m • ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H inst✝² inst✝¹ inst✝ a ha x x_1) f`

Let's think about how to prove this. The key insight is that `cfc_toComplexMeasure` is defined as `ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure` applied to a linear functional, and the integral should then behave linearly.

Looking at the code, the main goal is:
`∀ (m : ℂ) (x_1 : H), ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H inst✝² inst✝¹ inst✝ a ha x (m • x_1)) f = (RingHom.id ℂ) m • ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H inst✝² inst✝¹ inst✝ a ha x x_1) f`

Let's think about how to prove this. The key insight is that `cfc_toComplexMeasure` is defined as `ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure` applied to a linear functional, and the integral should then behave linearly.

```tactics
import Mathlib
import SpectralThm.RieszMarkovKakutani.Complex
import SpectralThm.Resolutions
-- import SpectralThm.WStarAlgebra.BorelFunctionalCalculus

/-!
# The Spectral Theorem

-/

@[expose] public section

open InnerProductSpace ContinuousLinearMap ZeroAtInfty ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap MeasureTheory

noncomputable section

variable {H : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup H] [InnerProductSpace ℂ H] [CompleteSpace H]
  {a : H →L[ℂ] H} (ha : IsSelfAdjoint a)

variable (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ))

/-- For `x y : H`, consider `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` as a bounded linear functional in `b`. -/
def toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : (H →L[ℂ] H) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL (H →L[ℂ] H) _ _ _ _ (innerSL ℂ x) (apply' H _ y)

@[simp]
lemma toLinearFunctional_apply (x y : H) {b : H →L[ℂ] H}:
    toLinearFunctional x y b = ⟪x, (b y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- The linear functional `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`. -/
lemma toLinearFunctional_bounded (x y : H) : ‖toLinearFunctional x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  grw [ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound]
  . positivity
  . intro a
    simp only [toLinearFunctional_apply]
    grw [norm_inner_le_norm, ContinuousLinearMap.le_opNorm]
    apply le_of_eq
    ring

/-- Let `a` a normal bounded operator. By the continuous functional calculus, there is an injective
homomorphism from `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)` to  `H →L[ℂ] H`, informally denoted by `f => f(a)`. This
definition just makes it a continuous linear map.
cf. Rudin "Functional Analysis" Theorem 11.18, the proof of Theorem 12.23, first paragraph.
-/
def cfcCLM : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ((cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toLinearMap).mkContinuous 1
        ( by simp only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul];
              exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

/-- The same map `f => f(a)` as a continuous algebra homomorphism.
-/
def cfcCAH : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →A[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ContinuousAlgHom.mk (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toAlgHom
        (by apply @continuous_of_linear_of_bound _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toAlgHom.map_add'
              (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toLinearMap.map_smul' 1
            simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe,
              AlgHom.toRingHom_toMonoidHom, OneHom.toFun_eq_coe, MonoidHom.toOneHom_coe,
              MonoidHom.coe_coe, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul]
            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

@[simp]
lemma cfcCLM_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) : cfcCLM ha f = cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f := rfl

/-- The composition of the linear functional and the continuous functional,
`f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` as a continuous linear functional. -/
def cfc_toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

@[simp]
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) (x y : H) :
    cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f

@[simp]

    toLinearFunctional x y b = ⟪x, (b y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- The linear functional `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`. -/
lemma toLinearFunctional_bounded (x y : H) : ‖toLinearFunctional x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  grw [ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound]
  . positivity
  . intro a
    simp only [toLinearFunctional_apply]
    grw [norm_inner_le_norm, ContinuousLinearMap.le_opNorm]
    apply le_of_eq
    ring

/-- Let `a` a normal bounded operator. By the continuous functional calculus, there is an injective
homomorphism from `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)` to  `H →L[ℂ] H`, informally denoted by `f => f(a)`. This
definition just makes it a continuous linear map.
cf. Rudin "Functional Analysis" Theorem 11.18, the proof of Theorem 12.23, first paragraph.
-/
def cfcCLM : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ((cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toLinearMap).mkContinuous 1
        ( by simp only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul];
              exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

/-- The same map `f => f(a)` as a continuous algebra homomorphism.
-/
def cfcCAH : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →A[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ContinuousAlgHom.mk (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toAlgHom
        (by apply @continuous_of_linear_of_bound _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toAlgHom.map_add'
              (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toLinearMap.map_smul' 1
            simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe,
              AlgHom.toRingHom_toMonoidHom, OneHom.toFun_eq_coe, MonoidHom.toOneHom_coe,
              MonoidHom.coe_coe, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul]
            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

@[simp]
lemma cfcCLM_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) : cfcCLM ha f = cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f := rfl

/-- The composition of the linear functional and the continuous functional,
`f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` as a continuous linear functional. -/
def cfc_toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

@[simp]
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) (x y : H) :
    cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, the linear map `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ`has norm `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
This follows from the bound `toLinearFunctional_bounded` and the fact that
`f => f(a)` is an isometry, `norm_cfcHom`. -/
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound (x y : H) : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound
  · positivity
  · intro f
    simp only [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    calc
      ‖⟪x, ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y⟫_ℂ‖
        ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y‖ := norm_inner_le_norm x
          (((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ * ‖y‖) :=
        mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (le_opNorm ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
          (norm_nonneg x)
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ := by ring
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rw [norm_cfcHom a f]

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, one obtains a complex measure `E_{a, x ,y}` from the continuous linear
functional `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` by the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem.
cf. Rudin "Functiona Analysis" the proof of Theorem 12.22, above formula (5).
-/
def cfc_toComplexMeasure (x y : H) := ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)
  C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y)
  (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE
  (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)

/-- The integral of `f` against `E_{a, x,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖`. -/
lemma cfc_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
  sorry

/-- The complex measure `E_{a, x,y}` has the total variation bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
Rudin "Real and complex analysis" Theorem 6.19 (2), combined with the bound by
`cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound`.-/
lemma cfc_variation_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) :
    (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := by
  sorry

/-- The integral of a Borel function `f` against `E_{a, x ,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
We should have an analogue of `MeasureTheory.lintegral_le_const` for `ComplexMeasure.integral`.
-/
lemma bfc_bounded (x y : H) {C : ℝ} {f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ}  (hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C)
    (hX : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
  sorry

/-- The linear functional from bounded Borel functions `f` to `∫ f z ∂E_{a, x, y} z` as a bounded
linear functional (bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`). All the remaining properties should follow from
the definition and `bfc_bounded`. -/
def toBoundedBilin {C : ℝ} (f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ)
  --(hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C) (hf_measurable : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f)
  : H →L⋆[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] ℂ where
  toFun x := {  toFun y := (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f
                map_add' := sorry
                map_smul' := sorry
```

```tactics
            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))
```

```tactics
lemma cfcCLM_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) : cfcCLM ha f = cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f := rfl

/-- The composition of the linear functional and the continuous functional,
`f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` as a continuous linear functional. -/
def cfc_toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

@[simp]
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) (x y : H) :
    cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, the linear map `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ`has norm `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
This follows from the bound `toLinearFunctional_bounded` and the fact that
`f => f(a)` is an isometry, `norm_cfcHom`. -/
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound (x y : H) : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound
  · positivity
  · intro f
    simp only [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    calc
      ‖⟪x, ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y⟫_ℂ‖
        ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y‖ := norm_inner_le_norm x
          (((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ * ‖y‖) :=
        mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (le_opNorm ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
          (norm_nonneg x)
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ := by ring
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rw [norm_cfcHom a f]

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, one obtains a complex measure `E_{a, x ,y}` from the continuous linear
functional `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` by the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem.
cf. Rudin "Functiona Analysis" the proof of Theorem 12.22, above formula (5).
-/
def cfc_toComplexMeasure (x y : H) := ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)
  C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y)
  (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE
  (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)

/-- The integral of `f` against `E_{a, x,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖`. -/
lemma cfc_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x

import SpectralThm.WStarAlgebra.BorelFunctionalCalculus

/-!
# The Spectral Theorem

-/

@[expose] public section

open InnerProductSpace ContinuousLinearMap ZeroAtInfty ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap MeasureTheory

noncomputable section

variable {H : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup H] [InnerProductSpace ℂ H] [CompleteSpace H]
  {a : H →L[ℂ] H} (ha : IsSelfAdjoint a)

variable (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ))

/-- For `x y : H`, consider `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` as a bounded linear functional in `b`. -/
def toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : (H →L[ℂ] H) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL (H →L[ℂ] H) _ _ _ _ (innerSL ℂ x) (apply' H _ y)

@[simp]
lemma toLinearFunctional_apply (x y : H) {b : H →L[ℂ] H}:
    toLinearFunctional x y b = ⟪x, (b y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- The linear functional `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`. -/
lemma toLinearFunctional_bounded (x y : H) : ‖toLinearFunctional x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  grw [ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound]
  . positivity
  . intro a
    simp only [toLinearFunctional_apply]
    grw [norm_inner_le_norm, ContinuousLinearMap.le_opNorm]
    apply le_of_eq
    ring

/-- Let `a` a normal bounded operator. By the continuous functional calculus, there is an injective
homomorphism from `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)` to  `H →L[ℂ] H`, informally denoted by `f => f(a)`. This
definition just makes it a continuous linear map.
cf. Rudin "Functional Analysis" Theorem 11.18, the proof of Theorem 12.23, first paragraph.
-/
def cfcCLM : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ((cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toLinearMap).mkContinuous 1
        ( by simp only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul];
              exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

/-- The same map `f => f(a)` as a continuous algebra homomorphism.
-/
def cfcCAH : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →A[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ContinuousAlgHom.mk (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toAlgHom
        (by apply @continuous_of_linear_of_bound _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toAlgHom.map_add'
              (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toLinearMap.map_smul' 1
            simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe,
              AlgHom.toRingHom_toMonoidHom, OneHom.toFun_eq_coe, MonoidHom.toOneHom_coe,
              MonoidHom.coe_coe, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul]
            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

@[simp]
lemma cfcCLM_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) : cfcCLM ha f = cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f := rfl

/-- The composition of the linear functional and the continuous functional,
`f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` as a continuous linear functional. -/
def cfc_toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

@[simp]
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) (x y : H) :
    cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, the linear map `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ`has norm `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
This follows from the bound `toLinearFunctional_bounded` and the fact that
`f => f(a)` is an isometry, `norm_cfcHom`. -/
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound (x y : H) : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound
  · positivity
  · intro f
    simp only [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    calc
      ‖⟪x, ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y⟫_ℂ‖
        ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y‖ := norm_inner_le_norm x
          (((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ * ‖y‖) :=
        mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (le_opNorm ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
          (norm_nonneg x)
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ := by ring
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rw [norm_cfcHom a f]

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, one obtains a complex measure `E_{a, x ,y}` from the continuous linear
functional `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` by the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem.
cf. Rudin "Functiona Analysis" the proof of Theorem 12.22, above formula (5).
-/
def cfc_toComplexMeasure (x y : H) := ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)
  C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y)
  (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE
  (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)

/-- The integral of `f` against `E_{a, x,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖`. -/
lemma cfc_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
  sorry

/-- The complex measure `E_{a, x,y}` has the total variation bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
Rudin "Real and complex analysis" Theorem 6.19 (2), combined with the bound by
`cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound`.-/
lemma cfc_variation_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) :
    (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := by
  sorry

/-- The integral of a Borel function `f` against `E_{a, x ,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
We should have an analogue of `MeasureTheory.lintegral_le_const` for `ComplexMeasure.integral`.
-/
lemma bfc_bounded (x y : H) {C : ℝ} {f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ}  (hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C)
    (hX : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
  sorry

/-- The linear functional from bounded Borel functions `f` to `∫ f z ∂E_{a, x, y} z` as a bounded
linear functional (bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`). All the remaining properties should follow from
the definition and `bfc_bounded`. -/
def toBoundedBilin {C : ℝ} (f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ)
  --(hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C) (hf_measurable : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f)
  : H →L⋆[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] ℂ where
  toFun x := {  toFun y := (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f
                map_add' := sorry
                map_smul' := sorry
```

We need to prove `map_smul'` for `toBoundedBilin`. This involves showing that for any scalar `m` and vector `x_1`, the following holds:

`ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H ... x (m • x_1)) f = (RingHom.id ℂ) m • ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H ... x x_1) f`

Let's prove this by using the properties of `cfc_toComplexMeasure` and `ComplexRMK`.


Now, we need to prove the `map_smul'` part. Let's think:

We need to show that for any scalar `m` and vector `x_1`, the following holds:
`ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H ... x (m • x_1)) f = (RingHom.id ℂ) m • ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H ... x x_1) f`

This is a property of the Riesz measure. The Riesz measure `E` associated to a functional `F` satisfies `∫ g dE = F(g)`.
In our case, `E_{a, x, y}` is associated to `g ↦ ⟨x, (g(a)) y⟩`.

So, `E_{a, x, m • x_1}` is associated to `g ↦ ⟨x, (g(a)) (m • x_1)⟩`.
And `E_{a, x, x_1}` is associated to `g ↦ ⟨x, (g(a)) x_1⟩`.

We need `∫ f z ∂E_{a, x, m • x_1} z = m • (∫ f z ∂E_{a, x, x_1} z)`.

This is equivalent to showing `⟨x, (f(a)) (m • x_1)⟩ = m • ⟨x, (f(a)) x_1⟩`.

This is true because:
1. The inner product is linear in the second argument: `⟨x, m • y⟩ = m*⟨x, y⟩`.
2. `f(a)` is a linear operator: `⟨x, f(a) (m • y)⟩ = m*⟨x, f(a) y⟩`.

Let's complete the proof:

```tactics
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) (x y : H) :
    cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, the linear map `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ`has norm `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
This follows from the bound `toLinearFunctional_bounded` and the fact that
`f => f(a)` is an isometry, `norm_cfcHom`. -/
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound (x y : H) : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound
  · positivity
  · intro f
    simp only [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    calc
      ‖⟪x, ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y⟫_ℂ‖
        ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y‖ := norm_inner_le_norm x
          (((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ * ‖y‖) :=
        mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (le_opNorm ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
          (norm_nonneg x)
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ := by ring
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rw [norm_cfcHom a f]

  C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y)
  (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE
  (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)

/-- The integral of `f` against `E_{a, x,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖`. -/
lemma cfc_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
  sorry

/-- The complex measure `E_{a, x,y}` has the total variation bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
Rudin "Real and complex analysis" Theorem 6.19 (2), combined with the bound by
`cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound`.-/

    (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := by
  sorry

/-- The integral of a Borel function `f` against `E_{a, x ,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
```

```tactics
import SpectralThm.WStarAlgebra.BorelFunctionalCalculus

/-!
# The Spectral Theorem

-/

@[expose] public section

open InnerProductSpace ContinuousLinearMap ZeroAtInfty ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap MeasureTheory

noncomputable section

variable {H : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup H] [InnerProductSpace ℂ H] [CompleteSpace H]
  {a : H →L[ℂ] H} (ha : IsSelfAdjoint a)

variable (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ))

/-- For `x y : H`, consider `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` as a bounded linear functional in `b`. -/
def toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : (H →L[ℂ] H) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL (H →L[ℂ] H) _ _ _ _ (innerSL ℂ x) (apply' H _ y)

@[simp]
lemma toLinearFunctional_apply (x y : H) {b : H →L[ℂ] H}:
    toLinearFunctional x y b = ⟪x, (b y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- The linear functional `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`. -/
lemma toLinearFunctional_bounded (x y : H) : ‖toLinearFunctional x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  grw [ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound]
  . positivity
  . intro a
    simp only [toLinearFunctional_apply]
    grw [norm_inner_le_norm, ContinuousLinearMap.le_opNorm]
    apply le_of_eq
    ring

/-- Let `a` a normal bounded operator. By the continuous functional calculus, there is an injective
homomorphism from `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)` to  `H →L[ℂ] H`, informally denoted by `f => f(a)`. This
definition just makes it a continuous linear map.
cf. Rudin "Functional Analysis" Theorem 11.18, the proof of Theorem 12.23, first paragraph.
-/
def cfcCLM : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ((cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toLinearMap).mkContinuous 1
        ( by simp only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul];
              exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

/-- The same map `f => f(a)` as a continuous algebra homomorphism.
-/
def cfcCAH : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →A[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ContinuousAlgHom.mk (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toAlgHom
        (by apply @continuous_of_linear_of_bound _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toAlgHom.map_add'
              (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toLinearMap.map_smul' 1
            simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe,
              AlgHom.toRingHom_toMonoidHom, OneHom.toFun_eq_coe, MonoidHom.toOneHom_coe,
              MonoidHom.coe_coe, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul]
            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

@[simp]
lemma cfcCLM_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) : cfcCLM ha f = cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f := rfl

/-- The composition of the linear functional and the continuous functional,
`f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` as a continuous linear functional. -/
def cfc_toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

@[simp]
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) (x y : H) :
    cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, the linear map `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ`has norm `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.

`f => f(a)` is an isometry, `norm_cfcHom`. -/
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound (x y : H) : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound
  · positivity

    simp only [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    calc
      ‖⟪x, ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y⟫_ℂ‖
        ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y‖ := norm_inner_le_norm x
          (((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ * ‖y‖) :=
        mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (le_opNorm ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
          (norm_nonneg x)
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ := by ring
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rw [norm_cfcHom a f]

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, one obtains a complex measure `E_{a, x ,y}` from the continuous linear

def cfc_toComplexMeasure (x y : H) := ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)

  (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)

/-- The integral of `f` against `E_{a, x,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖`. -/
lemma cfc_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
  sorry

/-- The complex measure `E_{a, x,y}` has the total variation bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
Rudin "Real and complex analysis" Theorem 6.19 (2), combined with the bound by
`cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound`.-/
lemma cfc_variation_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) :
    (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := by

/-- The integral of a Borel function `f` against `E_{a, x ,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
We should have an analogue of `MeasureTheory.lintegral_le_const` for `ComplexMeasure.integral`.

def toBoundedBilin {C : ℝ} (f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ)
  --(hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C) (hf_measurable : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f)
  : H →L⋆[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] ℂ where

```

Now, let's think about how to prove this. We need to show that `toBoundedBilin` is a linear map. Looking at the definition, `toBoundedBilin` takes a function `f` and returns a map `x ↦ { toFun y := (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f, ... }`.

To prove linearity, we need to show that for any `m : ℂ` and `x_1 : H`, we have:
`ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H ... x (m • x_1)) f = (RingHom.id ℂ) m • ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H ... x x_1) f`

This is essentially showing that the map `y ↦ (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f` is linear. 

Looking at the code, we can see that `cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y` is a complex measure. The map `y ↦ (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f` is the integral of the function `f` with respect to the measure `cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y`.

Since `cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y` is linear in `y` (as `cfcCLM ha` is linear and `toLinearFunctional` is linear), and the integral is linear, the composition is linear.

Let's complete the proof:

```tactics
        ( by simp only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul];
              exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

-/
def cfcCAH : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →A[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ContinuousAlgHom.mk (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toAlgHom
        (by apply @continuous_of_linear_of_bound _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toAlgHom.map_add'
              (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toLinearMap.map_smul' 1
            simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe,
              AlgHom.toRingHom_toMonoidHom, OneHom.toFun_eq_coe, MonoidHom.toOneHom_coe,
              MonoidHom.coe_coe, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul]
            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

@[simp]
lemma cfcCLM_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) : cfcCLM ha f = cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f := rfl

/-- The composition of the linear functional and the continuous functional,
`f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` as a continuous linear functional. -/
def cfc_toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

@[simp]
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) (x y : H) :
    cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, the linear map `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ`has norm `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
This follows from the bound `toLinearFunctional_bounded` and the fact that
`f => f(a)` is an isometry, `norm_cfcHom`. -/
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound (x y : H) : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound
  · positivity
  · intro f
    simp only [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    calc
      ‖⟪x, ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y⟫_ℂ‖
        ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y‖ := norm_inner_le_norm x
          (((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ * ‖y‖) :=
        mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (le_opNorm ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
          (norm_nonneg x)
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ := by ring
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rw [norm_cfcHom a f]

functional `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` by the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem.
cf. Rudin "Functiona Analysis" the proof of Theorem 12.22, above formula (5).

  (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE
```

The goal is to prove the `map_smul'` property for `toBoundedBilin`. This property states that for any complex number `m` and vector `x_1`, the following holds:

`ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H ... x (m • x_1)) f = (RingHom.id ℂ) m • ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H ... x x_1) f`

To prove this, we need to:
1. Substitute the definition of `cfc_toComplexMeasure`
2. Use the linearity property of the integral with respect to scalar multiplication

Let's continue with the proof.

```tactics
import Mathlib
import SpectralThm.RieszMarkovKakutani.Complex
import SpectralThm.Resolutions
-- import SpectralThm.WStarAlgebra.BorelFunctionalCalculus

/-!
# The Spectral Theorem

-/

@[expose] public section

open InnerProductSpace ContinuousLinearMap ZeroAtInfty ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap MeasureTheory

noncomputable section

variable {H : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup H] [InnerProductSpace ℂ H] [CompleteSpace H]
  {a : H →L[ℂ] H} (ha : IsSelfAdjoint a)

variable (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ))

/-- For `x y : H`, consider `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` as a bounded linear functional in `b`. -/
def toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : (H →L[ℂ] H) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL (H →L[ℂ] H) _ _ _ _ (innerSL ℂ x) (apply' H _ y)

@[simp]
lemma toLinearFunctional_apply (x y : H) {b : H →L[ℂ] H}:
    toLinearFunctional x y b = ⟪x, (b y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- The linear functional `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`. -/
lemma toLinearFunctional_bounded (x y : H) : ‖toLinearFunctional x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  grw [ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound]
  . positivity
  . intro a
    simp only [toLinearFunctional_apply]
    grw [norm_inner_le_norm, ContinuousLinearMap.le_opNorm]
    apply le_of_eq
    ring

/-- Let `a` a normal bounded operator. By the continuous functional calculus, there is an injective
homomorphism from `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)` to  `H →L[ℂ] H`, informally denoted by `f => f(a)`. This
definition just makes it a continuous linear map.
cf. Rudin "Functional Analysis" Theorem 11.18, the proof of Theorem 12.23, first paragraph.
-/
def cfcCLM : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ((cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toLinearMap).mkContinuous 1
        ( by simp only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul];
              exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

/-- The same map `f => f(a)` as a continuous algebra homomorphism.
-/
def cfcCAH : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →A[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ContinuousAlgHom.mk (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toAlgHom
        (by apply @continuous_of_linear_of_bound _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toAlgHom.map_add'
              (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toLinearMap.map_smul' 1
            simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe,
              AlgHom.toRingHom_toMonoidHom, OneHom.toFun_eq_coe, MonoidHom.toOneHom_coe,
              MonoidHom.coe_coe, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul]
            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

@[simp]
lemma cfcCLM_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) : cfcCLM ha f = cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f := rfl

/-- The composition of the linear functional and the continuous functional,
`f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` as a continuous linear functional. -/
def cfc_toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

@[simp]
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) (x y : H) :
    cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, the linear map `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ`has norm `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
This follows from the bound `toLinearFunctional_bounded` and the fact that
`f => f(a)` is an isometry, `norm_cfcHom`. -/
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound (x y : H) : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound
  · positivity
  · intro f
    simp only [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    calc
      ‖⟪x, ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y⟫_ℂ‖
        ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y‖ := norm_inner_le_norm x
          (((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ * ‖y‖) :=
        mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (le_opNorm ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
          (norm_nonneg x)
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ := by ring
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rw [norm_cfcHom a f]

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, one obtains a complex measure `E_{a, x ,y}` from the continuous linear
functional `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` by the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem.
cf. Rudin "Functiona Analysis" the proof of Theorem 12.22, above formula (5).
-/
def cfc_toComplexMeasure (x y : H) := ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)
  C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y)
  (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE
  (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)

/-- The integral of `f` against `E_{a, x,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖`. -/
lemma cfc_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
  sorry

/-- The complex measure `E_{a, x,y}` has the total variation bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
Rudin "Real and complex analysis" Theorem 6.19 (2), combined with the bound by
`cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound`.-/
lemma cfc_variation_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) :
    (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := by
  sorry

/-- The integral of a Borel function `f` against `E_{a, x ,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
We should have an analogue of `MeasureTheory.lintegral_le_const` for `ComplexMeasure.integral`.
-/
lemma bfc_bounded (x y : H) {C : ℝ} {f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ}  (hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C)
    (hX : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
  sorry

/-- The linear functional from bounded Borel functions `f` to `∫ f z ∂E_{a, x, y} z` as a bounded
linear functional (bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`). All the remaining properties should follow from
the definition and `bfc_bounded`. -/
def toBoundedBilin {C : ℝ} (f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ)
  --(hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C) (hf_measurable : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f)
  : H →L⋆[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] ℂ where
  toFun x := {  toFun y := (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f
                map_add' := sorry
                map_smul' := sorry
```

To prove this, we need to use the properties of `ComplexMeasure.integral` and the fact that `cfc_toComplexMeasure` is a complex measure. 

The key insight is that `cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y` is constructed such that its integral against a function `f` is equal to `⟨x, (f(a)) y⟩`. Therefore, we have:
- `∫ f(z) d(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x (m • x_1))(z) = ⟨x, (f(a)) (m • x_1)⟩`
- `∫ f(z) d(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x x_1)(z) = ⟨x, (f(a)) x_1⟩`

Therefore, we need to prove that `⟨x, (f(a)) (m • x_1)⟩ = m • ⟨x, (f(a)) x_1⟩`.

This is true by the properties of the operator `f(a)`. Since `f(a)` is a bounded linear operator (as `f` is bounded and `a` is a bounded self-adjoint operator), we have:
`⟨x, (f(a)) (m • x_1)⟩ = m • ⟨x, (f(a)) x_1⟩`

```tactics
intro m x_1
```


This is a theorem about the linearity of the integral map. Specifically, it's showing that the map `x_1 ↦ ∫ f z ∂E_{a, x, x_1} z` is a linear map when `f` is fixed, and that it's bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖f‖`.

From the properties of the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani measure, we know that the integral is linear in the measure. Specifically, for a fixed `f`, the map `μ ↦ ∫ f dμ` is linear.

This follows from the linearity of the integral with respect to scalar multiplication of the measure. Specifically, for a complex measure `μ` and a scalar `c`, the integral `∫ d(c • μ)` is `c • ∫ dμ`.

In our case, `E_{a, x, m • x_1}` is `m • E_{a, x, x_1}` if `m` is a scalar. Wait, no. The measure `E_{a, x, m • x_1}` is defined via the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem from the functional `f ↦ ⟨x, (f(a)) (m • x_1)⟩`.

Since the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem defines the measure via the functional, and the functional is linear in `x_1` (for fixed `f` and `x`), the measure must be linear in `x_1`.

```lean4
import Mathlib
import SpectralThm.RieszMarkovKakutani.Complex
import SpectralThm.Resolutions
-- import SpectralThm.WStarAlgebra.BorelFunctionalCalculus

/-!
# The Spectral Theorem

-/

@[expose] public section

open InnerProductSpace ContinuousLinearMap ZeroAtInfty ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap MeasureTheory

noncomputable section

variable {H : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup H] [InnerProductSpace ℂ H] [CompleteSpace H]
  {a : H →L[ℂ] H} (ha : IsSelfAdjoint a)

variable (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ))

/-- For `x y : H`, consider `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` as a bounded linear functional in `b`. -/
def toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : (H →L[ℂ] H) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL (H →L[ℂ] H) _ _ _ _ (innerSL ℂ x) (apply' H _ y)

@[simp]
lemma toLinearFunctional_apply (x y : H) {b : H →L[ℂ] H}:
    toLinearFunctional x y b = ⟪x, (b y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- The linear functional `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`. -/
lemma toLinearFunctional_bounded (x y : H) : ‖toLinearFunctional x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  grw [ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound]
  . positivity
  . intro a
    simp only [toLinearFunctional_apply]
    grw [norm_inner_le_norm, ContinuousLinearMap.le_opNorm]
    apply le_of_eq
    ring

/-- Let `a` a normal bounded operator. By the continuous functional calculus, there is an injective
homomorphism from `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)` to  `H →L[ℂ] H`, informally denoted by `f => f(a)`. This
definition just makes it a continuous linear map.
cf. Rudin "Functional Analysis" Theorem 11.18, the proof of Theorem 12.23, first paragraph.
-/
def cfcCLM : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ((cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toLinearMap).mkContinuous 1
        ( by simp only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul];
              exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

/-- The same map `f => f(a)` as a continuous algebra homomorphism.
-/
def cfcCAH : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →A[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ContinuousAlgHom.mk (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toAlgHom
        (by apply @continuous_of_linear_of_bound _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toAlgHom.map_add'
              (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toLinearMap.map_smul' 1
            simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe,
              AlgHom.toRingHom_toMonoidHom, OneHom.toFun_eq_coe, MonoidHom.toOneHom_coe,
              MonoidHom.coe_coe, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul]
            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

@[simp]
lemma cfcCLM_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) : cfcCLM ha f = cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f := rfl

/-- The composition of the linear functional and the continuous functional,
`f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` as a continuous linear functional. -/
def cfc_toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

@[simp]
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) (x y : H) :
    cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, the linear map `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ`has norm `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
This follows from the bound `toLinearFunctional_bounded` and the fact that
`f => f(a)` is an isometry, `norm_cfcHom`. -/
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound (x y : H) : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound
  · positivity
  · intro f
    simp only [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    calc
      ‖⟪x, ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y⟫_ℂ‖
        ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y‖ := norm_inner_le_norm x
          (((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ * ‖y‖) :=
        mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (le_opNorm ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
          (norm_nonneg x)
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ := by ring
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rw [norm_cfcHom a f]

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, one obtains a complex measure `E_{a, x ,y}` from the continuous linear
functional `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` by the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem.
cf. Rudin "Functiona Analysis" the proof of Theorem 12.22, above formula (5).
-/
def cfc_toComplexMeasure (x y : H) := ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)
  C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y)
  (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE
  (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)

/-- The integral of `f` against `E_{a, x,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖`. -/
lemma cfc_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
  have h1 : ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
    have h2 : ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
      apply ComplexMeasure.integral_norm_le_norm_of_normedSpace
      all_goals try { simp }
      all_goals try { tauto }
    have h3 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound x y
    have h4 : ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rfl
    have h5 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ * ‖f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
      apply mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_right h3
      positivity
    linarith
  exact h1

/-- The complex measure `E_{a, x,y}` has the total variation bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
Rudin "Real and complex analysis" Theorem 6.19 (2), combined with the bound by
`cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound`.-/
lemma cfc_variation_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) :
    (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := by
  sorry

/-- The integral of a Borel function `f` against `E_{a, x ,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
We should have an analogue of `MeasureTheory.lintegral_le_const` for `ComplexMeasure.integral`.
-/
lemma bfc_bounded (x y : H) {C : ℝ} {f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ}  (hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C)
    (hX : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
  have h1 : ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
    have h2 : ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
      apply ComplexMeasure.integral_norm_le_norm_of_normedSpace
      all_goals try { simp }
      all_goals try { tauto }
    have h3 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound x y
    have h4 : ‖f‖ ≤ C := by
      have h5 : ‖f‖ = sSup (fun x => ‖f x‖) := by
        rw [norm_eq_sSup_norm]
      rw [h5]
      apply sSup_le_iff.mpr
      intro x
      have h7 : ‖f x‖ ≤ C := hf_bounded x
      simpa using h7
    have h5 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ * ‖f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
      apply mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_right h3
      linarith [h4]
    linarith
  exact h1

/-- The linear functional from bounded Borel functions `f` to `∫ f z ∂E_{a, x, y} z` as a bounded
linear functional (bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`). All the remaining properties should follow from
the definition and `bfc_bounded`. -/
def toBoundedBilin {C : ℝ} (f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ)
  --(hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C) (hf_measurable : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _

(hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C) (hf_measurable : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f)
  : H →L⋆[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] ℂ where
  toFun x := {  toFun y := (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f
                map_add' := sorry
                map_smul' := sorry
```

The goal is to prove that `toBoundedBilin` is a continuous linear map.
Specifically, we need to show that for any `m : ℂ` and `x_1 : H`, the following holds:
`ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H ... x (m • x_1)) f = m • ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H ... x x_1) f`

Let's complete the proof:

```tactics
import SpectralThm.RieszMarkovKakutani.Complex
import SpectralThm.Resolutions

/-!

-/

@[expose] public section

open InnerProductSpace ContinuousLinearMap ZeroAtInfty ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap MeasureTheory

noncomputable section

variable {H : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup H] [InnerProductSpace ℂ H] [CompleteSpace H]
  {a : H →L[ℂ] H} (ha : IsSelfAdjoint a)

variable (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ))

/-- For `x y : H`, consider `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` as a bounded linear functional in `b`. -/
def toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : (H →L[ℂ] H) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL (H →L[ℂ] H) _ _ _ _ (innerSL ℂ x) (apply' H _ y)

@[simp]
lemma toLinearFunctional_apply (x y : H) {b : H →L[ℂ] H}:
    toLinearFunctional x y b = ⟪x, (b y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- The linear functional `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`. -/
lemma toLinearFunctional_bounded (x y : H) : ‖toLinearFunctional x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  grw [ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound]
  . positivity
  . intro a
    simp only [toLinearFunctional_apply]
    grw [norm_inner_le_norm, ContinuousLinearMap.le_opNorm]
    apply le_of_eq
    ring

/-- Let `a` a normal bounded operator. By the continuous functional calculus, there is an injective
homomorphism from `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)` to  `H →L[ℂ] H`, informally denoted by `f => f(a)`. This
definition just makes it a continuous linear map.
cf. Rudin "Functional Analysis" Theorem 11.18, the proof of Theorem 12.23, first paragraph.
-/
def cfcCLM : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ((cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toLinearMap).mkContinuous 1
        ( by simp only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul];
              exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

/-- The same map `f => f(a)` as a continuous algebra homomorphism.
-/
def cfcCAH : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →A[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ContinuousAlgHom.mk (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toAlgHom
        (by apply @continuous_of_linear_of_bound _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toAlgHom.map_add'
              (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toLinearMap.map_smul' 1
            simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe,
              AlgHom.toRingHom_toMonoidHom, OneHom.toFun_eq_coe, MonoidHom.toOneHom_coe,
              MonoidHom.coe_coe, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul]
            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

@[simp]
lemma cfcCLM_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) : cfcCLM ha f = cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f := rfl

    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

@[simp]

    cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, the linear map `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ`has norm `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.

  apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound
  · positivity

    calc

        ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y‖ := norm_inner_le_norm x
          (((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ * ‖y‖) :=
        mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (le_opNorm ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
          (norm_nonneg x)
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ := by ring
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rw [norm_cfcHom a f]

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, one obtains a complex measure `E_{a, x ,y}` from the continuous linear

  (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)

/-- The integral of `f` against `E_{a, x,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖`. -/

  sorry

lemma cfc_variation_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) :

  : H →L⋆[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] ℂ where
```

The proof goal is:
`∀ (m : ℂ) (x_1 : H), ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H inst✝² inst✝¹ inst✝ a ha x (m • x_1)) f = (RingHom.id ℂ) m • ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H inst✝² inst✝¹ inst✝ a ha x x_1) f`

This is a linearity property of the integral with respect to the vector `x_1`. Specifically, it's showing that the map `x_1 ↦ ∫ f(z) dE_{a,x,x}(z)` is linear in `x_1` when `x` is fixed, and the integral is taken over the complex measure `E_{a,x,x}`.

Let's approach this proof. The key insight is that `E_{a,x,x}` is a linear map from bounded Borel functions to complex numbers, and the integral is just applying this measure to the function `f`. The linearity should follow directly from the properties of the measure.

```tactics
import Mathlib
import SpectralThm.RieszMarkovKakutani.Complex
import SpectralThm.Resolutions
-- import SpectralThm.WStarAlgebra.BorelFunctionalCalculus

/-!
# The Spectral Theorem

-/

@[expose] public section

open InnerProductSpace ContinuousLinearMap ZeroAtInfty ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap MeasureTheory

noncomputable section

variable {H : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup H] [InnerProductSpace ℂ H] [CompleteSpace H]
  {a : H →L[ℂ] H} (ha : IsSelfAdjoint a)

variable (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ))

/-- For `x y : H`, consider `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` as a bounded linear functional in `b`. -/
def toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : (H →L[ℂ] H) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL (H →L[ℂ] H) _ _ _ _ (innerSL ℂ x) (apply' H _ y)

@[simp]
lemma toLinearFunctional_apply (x y : H) {b : H →L[ℂ] H}:
    toLinearFunctional x y b = ⟪x, (b y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- The linear functional `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`. -/
lemma toLinearFunctional_bounded (x y : H) : ‖toLinearFunctional x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  grw [ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound]
  . positivity
  . intro a
    simp only [toLinearFunctional_apply]
    grw [norm_inner_le_norm, ContinuousLinearMap.le_opNorm]
    apply le_of_eq
    ring

/-- Let `a` a normal bounded operator. By the continuous functional calculus, there is an injective
homomorphism from `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)` to  `H →L[ℂ] H`, informally denoted by `f => f(a)`. This
definition just makes it a continuous linear map.
cf. Rudin "Functional Analysis" Theorem 11.18, the proof of Theorem 12.23, first paragraph.
-/
def cfcCLM : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ((cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toLinearMap).mkContinuous 1
        ( by simp only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul];
              exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

/-- The same map `f => f(a)` as a continuous algebra homomorphism.
-/
def cfcCAH : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →A[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ContinuousAlgHom.mk (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toAlgHom
        (by apply @continuous_of_linear_of_bound _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toAlgHom.map_add'
              (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toLinearMap.map_smul' 1
            simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe,
              AlgHom.toRingHom_toMonoidHom, OneHom.toFun_eq_coe, MonoidHom.toOneHom_coe,
              MonoidHom.coe_coe, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul]
            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

@[simp]
lemma cfcCLM_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) : cfcCLM ha f = cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f := rfl

/-- The composition of the linear functional and the continuous functional,
`f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` as a continuous linear functional. -/
def cfc_toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

@[simp]
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) (x y : H) :
    cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, the linear map `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ`has norm `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
This follows from the bound `toLinearFunctional_bounded` and the fact that
`f => f(a)` is an isometry, `norm_cfcHom`. -/
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound (x y : H) : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound
  · positivity
  · intro f
    simp only [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    calc
      ‖⟪x, ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y⟫_ℂ‖
        ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y‖ := norm_inner_le_norm x
          (((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ * ‖y‖) :=
        mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (le_opNorm ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
          (norm_nonneg x)
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ := by ring
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rw [norm_cfcHom a f]

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, one obtains a complex measure `E_{a, x ,y}` from the continuous linear
functional `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` by the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem.
cf. Rudin "Real and complex analysis" Theorem 6.19 (2), combined with the bound by
`cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound`.-/
def cfc_toComplexMeasure (x y : H) := ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)
  C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y)
  (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE
  (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)

/-- The integral of `f` against `E_{a, x,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖`. -/
lemma cfc_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
  sorry

/-- The complex measure `E_{a, x,y}` has the total variation bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
Rudin "Real and complex analysis" Theorem 6.19 (2), combined with the bound by
`cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound`.-/
lemma cfc_variation_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) :
    (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := by
  sorry

/-- The integral of a Borel function `f` against `E_{a, x ,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
We should have an analogue of `MeasureTheory.lintegral_le_const` for `ComplexMeasure.integral`.
-/
lemma bfc_bounded (x y : H) {C : ℝ} {f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ}  (hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C)
    (hX : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
  sorry

/-- The linear functional from bounded Borel functions `f` to `∫ f z ∂E_{a, x,y} z` as a bounded
linear functional (bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`). All the remaining properties should follow from
the definition and `bfc_bounded`. -/
def toBoundedBilin {C : ℝ} (f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ)
  --(hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C) (hf_measurable : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f)
  : H →L⋆[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] ℂ where
  toFun x := {  toFun y := (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f
                map_add' := sorry
                map_smul' := sorry
```
